title: Add "use secondary for accent" toggle to School metaobject
description:
Some schools have a primary color that's too dark for buttons/accents on PDP and other pages. Add a boolean field  to the School metaobject so we can control per-school whether  pulls from  or .

**Affected sections (all places that read  for accent/theming):**
-  —  on the product page
-  —  on the features banner
-  —  on collection pages (coming-soon + school-header + product grid)
-  —  on cart line items
-  — JS-based  for coming-soon fallback
-  — JSON school map color for badge text
-  — dot fallback color when no logo

**Approach:**
1. Add  boolean field to the School metaobject definition in Shopify Admin
2. Create a reusable snippet that resolves the accent color based on the toggle
3. Update all 7 files to use the resolved accent color instead of hardcoding 
4. Set the toggle for schools identified in the color reference table (Alabama, Auburn, Utah State, Maine, Charlotte, Appalachian State, Southern Miss)

title: Add  boolean field to School metaobject in Shopify Admin
description:
Manual step in Shopify Admin:

1. Go to Settings → Custom data → Metaobjects → School
2. Add a new field:
   - Name: "Use secondary for accent"
   - Handle: 
   - Type: True/False (boolean)
   - Description: "When enabled, PDP buttons and accent colors use the secondary color instead of primary"
3. Save the metaobject definition
4. Set to  for these schools (per the color reference table): Alabama, Auburn, Utah State, Maine, Charlotte, Appalachian State, Southern Miss

This must be done before the theme code changes can reference .

Example usage:


But since  creates an isolated scope, the snippet should instead be an  or the pattern should output the color value directly. Consider using  or outputting inline. Evaluate the best Liquid pattern for reuse.

title: FBT Section Redesign: Compact List Layout
description:
## Overview

Redesign the Frequently Bought Together section from oversized vertically-stacked product cards to a compact, integrated list-style layout matching the Homefield reference design.

## Current State

The FBT section () renders full  snippets (with image carousels, badges, quick-add buttons) in a horizontal/vertical flex layout with "+" signs between cards. On mobile, cards stack vertically at 280px width — taking up massive screen real estate and feeling "bolted on" rather than integrated.

The section uses a web component () that:
- Fetches complementary/related product recommendations via 
- Renders recommendations by fetching  and extracting  markup
- Manages checkbox state, total price calculation, and bulk add-to-cart

## Target State (Homefield Reference)

A compact list where each product is a **horizontal row**:
- **Left:** Checkbox (circular or square, filled when checked)
- **Center-left:** Small square product thumbnail (~60-70px)
- **Center-right:** Product title + price on one line
- **Below title:** Variant selector text (e.g., "XS / Deep Navy") — compact inline selector
- **Bottom of section:** "Total Price: .XX" + "ADD TO CART" button

No image carousels, no badges, no plus signs, no per-item add-to-cart buttons.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Each FBT item renders as a compact horizontal row (checkbox | thumbnail | title+price | variant)
2. Small square thumbnails (~60-70px), no image carousels
3. Inline variant selector per row (compact dropdown or text selector)
4. No "+" signs between items
5. All items checked by default; clicking checkbox toggles selection
6. "Total Price" and "ADD ALL X TO CART" button at bottom
7. Total price updates dynamically when items are checked/unchecked or variants change
8. No horizontal overflow at any viewport
9. Section still hidden until recommendations load (existing behavior preserved)
10. Existing JS logic for fetching recommendations, add-to-cart, and variant:change events preserved
11. E2E tests updated to match new DOM structure
12. Design QA passes at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), desktop (1440px)

title: Replace FBT product-card usage with compact row markup
description:
## What

Rewrite the Liquid template in  to render compact list rows instead of full product cards. This is the core structural change.

## Current Implementation

The section renders  for the current product (line 25), and the JS  method (line 262) fetches full product-card HTML via  and injects it.

## Target Structure

Each FBT row should follow this markup pattern:



## Changes Required

### In the Liquid template (current product):
- Replace  with the compact row markup
- Include a hidden  block with variant data (same format as current product-card)
- Include a hidden  for tracking selected variant
- Render a compact variant selector (a  element or custom dropdown showing combined option values like "XS / Deep Navy")
- Use  for the small thumbnail
- Remove the  "This item" span
- Remove the  wrapper

### In the JS  method:
- Instead of fetching  and extracting  markup, fetch product JSON from  
- OR continue fetching the section render but extract only the data needed (title, price, image, variants) and build the compact row markup in JS
- Build the compact row HTML string matching the structure above
- Remove the "+" separator elements ()

### Keep working:
- , ,  attributes (used by toggle/total logic)
-  JSON and  input (used by )
- The  custom event dispatch when variant selection changes

## Files to modify
-  (template + JS)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Current product renders as a compact row with thumbnail, title, price, variant selector
- Recommended products render as compact rows (no full product cards)
- No plus signs between items
- Variant data and variant-id input are present for price calculation
- Section still fetches recommendations and shows/hides correctly

title: Restyle FBT section CSS for compact list layout
description:
## What

Replace all CSS in the  block of  to style the new compact list layout. The current CSS targets , , , and full product card overrides — all of which are being replaced.

## Target Design (Homefield Reference)

- **Section:** Centered, max-width constrained, clean heading
- **Product list:** Vertical stack of rows, each separated by a subtle border/divider
- **Each row:** Horizontal flex layout — checkbox (left) | thumbnail (center-left, ~60-70px square) | details (right, flex-grow)
- **Details area:** Product title + price on one line, variant selector below in smaller/muted text
- **Checkbox:** Left-aligned, vertically centered in the row. Filled/dark when checked, outlined when unchecked
- **Footer:** "Total Price: .XX" right-aligned or centered, followed by full-width "ADD TO CART" button

## CSS to Write



## Responsive Behavior
- Mobile (< 768px): Full-width rows, full-width button
- Desktop: Max-width constrained, button doesn't need to be full width

## CSS to Remove
- ,  (replaced by )
-  (removed entirely)
-  (removed)  
-  override (no more product cards)
- The absolute-positioned checkbox styles (checkbox is now in-flow)
- The  stylesheet tag import at top of file

## Files to modify
-  ({% stylesheet %} block)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compact horizontal rows with checkbox | thumb | title+price | variant
- Subtle dividers between rows
- Checkbox in-flow (not absolutely positioned)
- Clean footer with total + button
- No horizontal overflow at any viewport
- Visually matches Homefield reference at mobile viewport

title: Add inline variant selector to FBT rows
description:
## What

Add a compact inline variant selector to each FBT row so users can pick size/color before bulk add-to-cart. The Homefield reference shows this as a simple text line like "XS / Deep Navy" — likely a native  element styled to look minimal.

## Implementation

### For the current product (Liquid-rendered):
In the row markup, add a  element populated from :



### For recommended products (JS-rendered):
When building the row HTML in , include a  built from the product's variants array (available from the product JSON or extracted from fetched section data).

### Variant change handler:
When the  changes:
1. Update the hidden  input value to the selected variant ID
2. Update the displayed price () to the selected variant's price
3. Dispatch a  event (already listened to by )

This replaces the complex dropdown system in the existing  snippet with a simple native .

## Styling
- Native  with minimal styling: no border or subtle border, small font size (~0.75-0.8rem), muted color
- Sits below the product title in the details area
- Match the "XS / Deep Navy" text appearance from the Homefield reference

## Files to modify
-  (Liquid template + JS + CSS)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each row with multiple variants shows a compact variant selector
- Single-variant products show the variant name as plain text
- Changing variant updates the price display and total
- The hidden variant-id input stays in sync for add-to-cart
- Unavailable variants are disabled in the selector

title: Update FBT e2e tests for compact list layout
description:
## What

Rewrite  to match the new compact list DOM structure. Many existing selectors target classes/elements that no longer exist (, , , , , ).

## Tests to Update

### Keep (update selectors):
1. **"section renders with recommendations"** — keep as-is (selector  unchanged)
2. **"has centered heading"** — keep  check, REMOVE  assertion (rule is removed)
3. **"shows current product plus 2 recommendations (3 items total)"** — update selector from  (still works if we keep that data attr)
4. **"all checkboxes are checked by default"** — keep ( still used)
5. **"clicking checkbox unchecks/re-checks"** — keep logic, verify selectors
6. **"total price and ADD ALL button are visible"** — keep as-is
7. **"no horizontal overflow"** — keep as-is

### Remove:
8. **"current product has 'This item' label"** — remove (label is gone)
9. **"plus signs appear between cards"** — remove (plus signs are gone)
10. **"each item uses the shared product-card snippet"** — remove (no more product-card)

### Add New:
11. **"each row has a thumbnail image"** — verify  exists in each row
12. **"each row shows product title and price"** — verify  and 
13. **"variant selector is present for multi-variant products"** — verify  or 
14. **"changing variant updates row price and total"** — select a different variant, verify price changes
15. **"rows have compact list layout (not card layout)"** — verify rows are narrow height, not oversized cards

## Files to modify
- 

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests pass against the new compact list layout
- No references to removed elements (, , , )
- New tests cover thumbnails, titles, prices, variant selectors
- Tests run successfully with

description:

## Changes

2. **Remove the section render fetch:**

3. **Verify  and  are still needed:**
